verses.text: Masters, give your servants what is just; 'ye also have a Master in heaven.' Just masters. They too answer to a higher Lord.
Continue in prayer, watching 'with thanksgiving'; pray a door of utterance be opened. Watchful prayer. He asks prayer for open doors for the word.
Continue in prayer, watching 'with thanksgiving'; pray a door of utterance be opened. Watchful prayer. He asks prayer for open doors for the word.
Continue in prayer, watching 'with thanksgiving'; pray a door of utterance be opened. Watchful prayer. He asks prayer for open doors for the word.
Walk in wisdom toward them without; 'let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt.' Gracious words. Speech to outsiders should be wise and kind.
Walk in wisdom toward them without; 'let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt.' Gracious words. Speech to outsiders should be wise and kind.
Tychicus and Onesimus shall make known my state, faithful and beloved brethren. Trusted men. He sends word by faithful friends.
Tychicus and Onesimus shall make known my state, faithful and beloved brethren. Trusted men. He sends word by faithful friends.
Tychicus and Onesimus shall make known my state, faithful and beloved brethren. Trusted men. He sends word by faithful friends.
Aristarchus, Marcus, and Justus salute you, fellowworkers and a comfort. Faithful few. These were a comfort to him in bonds.
Aristarchus, Marcus, and Justus salute you, fellowworkers and a comfort. Faithful few. These were a comfort to him in bonds.
Epaphras labours for you in prayers, 'that ye may stand perfect and complete.' Wrestling prayer. He strives for them in earnest intercession.
Epaphras labours for you in prayers, 'that ye may stand perfect and complete.' Wrestling prayer. He strives for them in earnest intercession.
Luke and Demas greet you; salute the brethren and the church in Nymphas' house. Greetings. The churches are bound in love.
Luke and Demas greet you; salute the brethren and the church in Nymphas' house. Greetings. The churches are bound in love.
Read this epistle also among the Laodiceans; 'Remember my bonds. Grace be with you.' Closing word. He ends recalling his chains and wishing grace.
Read this epistle also among the Laodiceans; 'Remember my bonds. Grace be with you.' Closing word. He ends recalling his chains and wishing grace.
